description_long: This object is a rectangular parcel locker unit with a matte black finish and a flat top surface. The front face is divided into nine square compartments, each marked with a large, bold white number from 1 to 9, suggesting individual lockers for parcel storage. The numbers are centered on each compartment door, which appear to be made of metal and are likely hinged or latched for secure access. The sides and back of the unit are plain, with no visible openings, handles, or signage. The overall construction appears robust and industrial, suitable for outdoor or semi-public urban environments. There are no visible digital interfaces, branding, or instructional signage on the exterior.
description: A matte black parcel locker with nine numbered compartments on the front, each with a bold white number. The sides and back are plain and unmarked, giving it a sturdy, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: The front view shows nine square compartments, each with a large white number (1-9) on a black background.
description_view_1: The left side view is plain and featureless, showing only the solid black side panel.
description_view_2: The rear view is entirely plain, with no visible openings, numbers, or features.
description_view_3: The right side view is also plain, but the compartment divisions are faintly visible along the edge.
attribute_access_door_design: swing-out metal doors with visible numbering and likely recessed or flush handles
